w:r.w:t: Working record of confirmed, tentative, and open product and architecture decisions after the latest recipe-layer update.

This revision keeps all previously locked stack choices and adds three explicit decisions from the latest project discussion: Groq llama-3.1-8b-instant is the main conversational LLM, the recipe layer is locked to retrieval-first grounded summary/light adaptation rather than default free-form generation, and Google Gemini 2.5 Flash is the stable multimodal vision model for the no-barcode flow.
Use nutrition.xlsx as the primary food nutrient dataset, Egyptian Food.csv for Egyptian foods, and FoodData Central as fallback
This gives the backend a simple primary lookup, localized Egyptian coverage, and broader fallback coverage; Egyptian Food.csv still requires schema, unit, provenance, and licensing validation.
Use Open Food Facts API as packaged-food barcode source
Use Google Gemini 2.5 Flash for foods with no barcode
Stable multimodal model locked; backend-owned structured JSON output contract required.
Retain mobile app + backend API + tool-using AI layer
Keep 12-week MVP with contract-first, parallel execution
Use contract-first APIs and stub-driven development
Still required to avoid cross-team blocking.
Use retrieval-first recipe assistance with grounded summary/light adaptation only
Option B locked; default free-form recipe generation not part of the current baseline.
Use Groq llama-3.1-8b-instant as the main conversational LLM
Selected for low cost, speed, and acceptable MVP capacity.
Tentative state removed in the prior revision.
Prefer retrieved structured data over model recall for nutrition answers
System must not present medical advice as diagnosis or treatment
Hard product boundary stays in force.
Egyptian food support is a first-class requirement
Localization is now core scope, not a later enhancement.
Lock backend API to Python + FastAPI
Framework choice now explicit rather than implied.
Flutter calls FastAPI only; no direct third-party API calls from the app
Protects secrets and centralizes normalization, caching, and vendor logic.
API keys and provider credentials remain backend-only
No secrets embedded in mobile builds.
Use the recipe dataset only inside the recipe layer
Dataset supports recipe retrieval, candidate ranking, grounded recipe assistance, and recipe discussion. It is not used in lookup or as a replacement nutrition source.
The system should retrieve grounded recipe candidates before any optional adaptation step.
Recipe discussion must use retrieved recipe context
Follow-up recipe answers should rely on retrieved recipe rows and structured match context, not free-form model recall.
Use Groq llama-3.1-8b-instant for recipe summary/light adaptation
Keeps one provider path for conversational Q&A and recipe adaptation.
Normalize Gemini vision output into a backend-defined structured JSON schema
Prevents upstream response drift from leaking into mobile or nutrition-estimation logic.
Use "Open Food Facts API" rather than older variants.
Use "Google Gemini 2.5 Flash" or API model id `gemini-2.5-flash`.
Use "recipe corpus" or "recipe dataset inside the recipe layer", not "lookup database".
Use "retrieval-first grounded recipe assistance with summary/light adaptation"; do not describe the current system as free-form recipe generation.
Use "Groq llama-3.1-8b-instant" as the locked conversational LLM.
• The backend must expose stable mobile-facing contracts even if upstream providers change.
• Barcode and non-barcode flows should remain separate internally because their confidence and failure modes differ.
• Gemini outputs should use a structured JSON contract with dish candidates, ingredients, confidence, and source fields.
• Prompting and safety rules should now be written against the locked providers: Groq llama-3.1-8b-instant for conversational and recipe adaptation, and Gemini 2.5 Flash for vision.

• The nutrition layer must reconcile packaged products and local meal estimates through one normalized schema and a backend-owned source hierarchy: nutrition.xlsx first, Egyptian Food.csv for Egyptian foods, then FoodData Central fallback.
• The recipe layer must keep recipe retrieval, candidate ranking, missing-ingredient logic, and recipe discussion grounded in the recipe corpus.
• Recipe discussion should explain why a recipe was suggested, what ingredients are missing, and how the candidate could be adapted without inventing a substantially new recipe by default.
Schema audit, unit consistency, provenance, and license review for Egyptian Food.csv, plus documented matching rules between nutrition.xlsx and FoodData Central fallback
Egyptian Food.csv cannot be treated as trusted without this, and the primary-to-fallback matching order must be documented before production rollout.
Canonical ingredient vocabulary, deduplication, dietary tags, and allergen/exclusion handling
Affects recipe quality, grounding quality, and discussion reliability.
Email/password vs OAuth vs guest mode, unless already decided elsewhere and not logged
Still affects backend scope and mobile onboarding flow if unresolved.
• Revise the PRD and sprint documents so they explicitly state Groq llama-3.1-8b-instant as the conversational LLM and recipe-adaptation model.
• Update the CV/API contracts to state Google Gemini 2.5 Flash as the locked multimodal vision model and to enforce a backend-defined JSON response schema.
• Document the backend-only integration rule in mobile and backend deliverables to prevent direct provider calls from the app.
• Add a formal dataset validation note before Egyptian Food.csv is treated as authoritative, and document nutrition.xlsx as the primary food nutrient source with FoodData Central as fallback.
• Finalize recipe corpus preprocessing standards: canonical ingredient vocabulary, deduplication, dietary tags, and exclusion handling.
• Document the recipe layer as retrieval-first grounded assistance with summary/light adaptation only; no default free-form recipe generation.
• Prior workflow plan and proposal artifacts.
• Previous architecture baseline and decision log.
• Latest team discussion locking Groq llama-3.1-8b-instant for conversational Q&A and recipe adaptation.
• Latest team discussion locking Option B for the recipe layer: retrieval-first grounded summary/light adaptation.
• Latest team discussion locking Google Gemini 2.5 Flash for the no-barcode vision flow.
